content.full_text:
Al Haynes 
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 ••• 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814 
Technical Assistance 
•• 
Administration 
•• 
executive/Legal 
(916) 322·5662 
322·5660 
322.5901 
March 30, 1984 
enforcement 
322-6441 
Watershed and Planning Analyst 
San Lorenzo Valley Water District 
13060 Central Avenue 
P.O. Box H 
Boulder Creek, CA 
95006 
Re: 
Your Request for Advice 
Our File No. A-84-068 
Dear Mr. Haynes: 
You requested advice from this office concerning you~ 
obligations as a public official under the conflict of interest 
provisions of the Political Reform Act.ll 
You are a Watershed and Planning Analyst for the San Lorenzo 
Valley Water District ("District"), and, as such, you report and 
make recommendations to the Board of Directors for the District. 
The District is in the process of implementing a wastewater 
management program which includes the installation and operation 
of a collection system and sewage treatment facilities for 
selected areas within the District. 
In addition, the Dis trict 
is implementing a similar program in selected areas outside the 
District. 
The selected areas have been divided into three 
classifications based on the severity of the problem and the 
required solutions: 
Class I: 
Parcels in areas with severe problems for which 
no on-site solution exists. It is proposed that 
these parcels will require the installation of 
collection systems and sewage treatment 
facilities. 
1/ Government Code Sections 81000-91014. 
All statutory 
references are to the Government Code. 


Al Haynes 
March 30, 1984 
Page 2 
Class II: 
Class III: 
Parcels in areas with severe problems, but for 
which on-site systems can be upgraded to be 
functional. 
Parcels in areas where problems are not so 
severe. 
You own real property within the District in a Class r area, 
which you use for your principal residence. 
You state that 
District decisions concerning the Class r areas will have a 
significant financial effect on the value of your property. 
You 
ask whether you may participate in these decisions. 
Government Code Section 87100 provides that no local public 
official shall make, participate in the making, or in any way 
attempt to use his official position to influence a governmental 
decision in which he knows or has reason to know he has a 
financial interest. 
An official has a financial interest in a decision 
within the meaning of Section 87100 if it is reasonably 
foreseeable that the decision will have a material 
financial effect, distinguishable from its effect on 
the public generally, on: 
* * * 
(b) 
Any real property in which the public 
official has a direct or indirect interest worth more 
than one thousand dollars ($1,000). 
(c) 
Any source of income, other than loans by a 
commercial lending institution in the regular course of 
business on terms available to the public without 
regard to official status, aggregating two hundred 
fifty dollars {$250} or more in value provided to, 
received by or promised to the public official within 
12 months prior to the time when the decision is made. 
* * * 
(Section 87103.) 
Since you have an interest in real property which will be 
materially affected by the District decisions on the plan, the 
sole issue is whether the effects on you are distinguishable 


Al Haynes 
March 30, 1984 
Page 3 
from the effects on the public generally or a significant 
segment thereof. 
Janis McLean of our office recently advised Mr. Frederick W. 
Nitz, President of the District's Board of Directors, concerning 
this situation. 
(Advice Letter A-83-284.) 
Like you, his 
residence is located in a Class I area, and the value of the 
property will be affected by District decisions on the waste 
water plan. 
We advised him that, even though District decisions 
implementing the plan in the Class I areas will have a material 
financial effect on his property and his income, all of the 
parcels in the Class I areas will be similarly affected. 
Accordingly, since the number of parcels constitutes a 
"significant segment" of the total parcels,Y Mr. Nitz can 
make, and participate in the making of, District decisions on 
the waste water plan. 
Our analysis and advice in your situation is the same. 
Your 
parcel of real property will be affected in substantially the 
same manner as a "significant segment" o~ the total parcels. 
You may thus participate in the making of District decisions on 
the waste water plan. 
If I can be of further assistance, please feel free to 
contact me at (916) 322-5901. 
DMF:plh 
Sincerely, 
/~ldtl_JJ't,~~~ 
Di ane Maut;; Fishburn 
Staf f Counsel 
Legal Division 
£/ All of the relevant statistics used to make this 
determination are cited in our letter to Mr. Nitz.

sections.question: You ask whether you may participate in these decisions.
sections.conclusion: Your parcel of real property will be affected in substantially the same manner as a "significant segment" of the total parcels. You may thus participate in the making of District decisions on the waste water plan.
sections.question_synthetic: Whether a Watershed and Planning Analyst for the San Lorenzo Valley Water District, who owns real property in a Class I area that will be materially affected by District wastewater management decisions, may participate in those decisions under the conflict of interest provisions of the Political Reform Act.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC concluded that the requestor may participate in District wastewater management decisions because his property will be affected in substantially the same manner as a significant segment of other parcels in the Class I area, meaning the effects are not distinguishable from those on the public generally or a significant segment thereof.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Standard advice letter format with clear question and conclusion sections. References prior related advice letter A-83-284 to Mr. Frederick Nitz. Footnotes cite Government Code sections and relevant statistics.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: You ask whether you may participate in these decisions.

CONCLUSION: Your parcel of real property will be affected in substantially the same manner as a "significant segment" of the total parcels. You may thus participate in the making of District decisions on the waste water plan.

embedding.summary: FPPC advises a San Lorenzo Valley Water District analyst that he may participate in District decisions regarding a wastewater management program affecting his Class I property because the decision will affect a significant segment of similarly-situated parcels, not just his property uniquely.
